title: Ninja Woodfire Hot-Smoked Salmon
description: Dry-brined salmon hot-smoked on the Ninja Woodfire at 225°F with cherry or apple pellets. The brine, rinse, and pellicle steps are what separate this from average smoked salmon.
prepTime: 10 minutes (plus 4–8 hours brining and 1–2 hours pellicle rest)
ingredients: 1–1.5 lbs salmon fillet, skin-on, pin bones removed (wild sockeye or coho for stated macros)
Cherry or apple wood pellets (for Ninja Woodfire)
instructions: Mix 3 tbsp kosher salt, 1 tbsp maple syrup, 1 tsp black pepper, and 1 tsp garlic powder into a dry brine. Coat the flesh side of the 1–1.5 lb salmon fillet generously.
Place the brined salmon skin-side down on a wire rack set over a sheet pan. Refrigerate uncovered for 4–8 hours. Liquid pooling under the fish is expected and correct.
Rinse the salmon briefly under cold water to remove excess salt, then pat completely dry with paper towels.

Return the salmon skin-side down to the wire rack and refrigerate uncovered for 1–2 hours to form the pellicle. The surface should feel tacky and slightly glossy — this is what smoke clings to. Wet fish will steam instead of smoke.

Fill the Ninja Woodfire pellet box with cherry or apple wood pellets. Select the SMOKER function and set temperature to 225°F. Allow 8–10 minutes for the unit to preheat and smoke to establish before adding food.
Place the 1–1.5 lb salmon fillet skin-side down on the grill grate. Do not flip at any point during cooking.
Smoke at 225°F for 45–60 minutes. Pull when internal temperature reaches 125–130°F for a silky, medium result. 140°F+ yields a firmer, fully cooked texture.
Rest the salmon for 5 minutes loosely tented with foil. Carry-over heat will add another 3–5°F.
notes: Macros assume wild sockeye or coho at ~5 oz per serving. Farmed Atlantic runs closer to 280–300 calories and 15–18g fat for the same portion — adjust logging accordingly. Cherry and apple pellets are ideal; avoid hickory or mesquite. The rinse-then-pellicle sequence is the most skipped and most important part — don't rush the fridge rest. Serve with capers, thinly sliced red onion, and 0% plain Greek yogurt as a crème fraîche stand-in. Excellent cold the next day on multigrain bread.
